summary: RESTful web services form the first major study section.
Security, UI, testing, and deployment extend the REST service into a production-oriented application path.
gRPC, observability, and GraphQL broaden the API landscape beyond REST.
REST API foundations belong to the RESTful web services section.
Resource and URI design is a core REST API concern.
HTTP methods and status codes define REST request actions and outcomes.
HATEOAS is presented as an important REST concept for hypermedia navigation.
ETag caching is part of REST API response optimization and validation.
Versioning belongs to REST API evolution and compatibility planning.
Spring provides the Java framework foundation for implementing the API examples.
Spring Boot builds on Spring to simplify API application setup and execution.
IoC and dependency injection are central Spring framework concepts.
The IoC container creates and wires beans as dependency-managed objects.
Controllers are part of the Spring MVC request handling model.
Service components are Spring-managed collaborators for application logic.
Repository components are Spring-managed persistence collaborators.
Spring REST controllers implement HTTP endpoints for REST APIs.
Controllers call service components to execute application logic behind endpoints.
Services use repositories to access persistence without embedding database mechanics in controllers.
OpenAPI design-first implementation is part of the RESTful web services section.
OpenAPI specifies REST contracts before the Spring implementation is written.
OpenAPI code generation produces API interfaces and supporting artifacts from the specification.
Generated Spring interfaces shape the controller implementation contract.
Global exception handling centralizes controller failure responses.
JPA persistence implements repository-backed database access.
JPA persistence is introduced while adding business logic and data access to the REST API.
Controllers can add hypermedia links to improve REST response navigation.
Controllers can add ETag response headers for cache validation.
Reactive API design is the fifth chapter topic within the RESTful web services part.
Spring WebFlux implements reactive API handling for asynchronous non-blocking REST services.
Reactive APIs use Reactive Streams concepts such as publisher, subscriber, subscription, and backpressure.
WebFlux controllers and pipelines use Mono and Flux as reactive value wrappers.
Reactive persistence keeps the database boundary non-blocking in a reactive API pipeline.
Existing REST controller ideas evolve into reactive controller or functional endpoint implementations.
Traditional repositories evolve to reactive repositories when the whole API flow must stay non-blocking.
Spring Security begins the security, UI, testing, and deployment section.
Spring Security protects REST endpoints before requests reach application handlers.
JWT is used as an access token in the Spring Security flow.
Refresh tokens renew expired JWT access tokens while keeping logout and renewal behavior separate.
The OAuth2 resource server approach authenticates bearer tokens through Spring Security filters.
JWT access tokens authenticate calls to protected REST URLs.
Roles and authorities limit which users can access selected REST endpoints.
JWT claims can carry role information used in authorization decisions.
API testing is a dedicated topic in the second section.
API testing validates REST endpoints manually and through automation.
Unit and integration tests are the core automated testing categories.
Controller behavior can be validated through automated tests as part of API testing.
Service behavior is a target for unit and integration tests.
Docker containerization is part of the deployment chapter.
Spring Boot services can be packaged into Docker images for consistent deployment.
Kubernetes deploys the containerized application image into a cluster environment.
Kubernetes deployment belongs to the deployment chapter after Docker image packaging.
gRPC starts the third section of alternative API styles and operations.
The material contrasts REST and gRPC as different API communication approaches.
gRPC uses Protocol Buffers to define service and message contracts.
gRPC server, client, and service behavior are tested in the gRPC development chapter.
ELK logging belongs to the services logging and tracing topic.
The graph covers adding logging and tracing changes around gRPC code paths.
Zipkin and Micrometer tracing belongs to the observability topic.
Distributed tracing tracks performance and request paths across service calls.
GraphQL chapters conclude the API style expansion section.
GraphQL is compared with REST as an alternative client-facing API style.
DataLoader-style solutions address GraphQL N+1 query problems.
Subscriptions are part of GraphQL API development beyond queries and mutations.
GraphQL queries, mutations, and subscriptions receive automated testing coverage.
The REST API implementation is migrated toward asynchronous reactive API design.
After REST, the material introduces gRPC as another API communication style.
After gRPC and observability, the material introduces GraphQL basics and implementation.
The source quality caveat applies to the whole graph because the private input was OCR-derived and not fully reviewed.
Practice summaries should be treated as paraphrased guidance rather than exact source wording.
Protocol and tooling summaries are public-safe but should not be treated as exact OCR quotes.
JWT 적용은 API 보안의 한 부분이며, 별도의 API 위험 점검 없이 보안이 완결됐다고 보면 안 된다.
DGS 기반 서버 흐름은 GraphQL API 스타일을 스프링 애플리케이션에서 구현하는 구체적 경로다.
GraphQL 서버 구현은 instrumentation과 테스트로 런타임 동작을 관찰 가능하게 만든다.
컨트롤러 계약은 DTO validation을 통해 외부 입력을 서비스 호출 전 제약한다.
컨트롤러는 예외를 problem detail 같은 공통 실패 응답 계약으로 변환한다.
서비스 트랜잭션 경계는 JPA aggregate persistence를 업무 작업 단위로 감싼다.
JPA 세부사항이 controller로 올라오면 service/persistence 책임이 흐려진다.
OpenAPI design-first 루프는 contract regression으로 문서와 구현 drift를 확인한다.
HATEOAS 링크와 ETag는 리소스 상태 전이와 조건부 요청을 함께 표현한다.
OAuth resource server 검증은 method authorization으로 업무 작업별 권한 경계를 보강한다.
API security risk review는 token 검증과 노출 데이터 범위를 반복 점검하게 한다.
slice test는 controller contract를 빠르게 검증하기 위해 Spring context 범위를 좁힌다.
계약 회귀 검증은 OpenAPI 계약이 구현 변경 후에도 유지되는지 확인한다.
container profile boundary는 Spring Boot 설정을 환경별 실행 조건으로 분리한다.
trace correlation은 controller 진입부터 서비스 처리까지 요청 흐름을 이어 본다.
gRPC contract는 REST resource 계약과 다른 IDL 기반 내부 통신 선택지로 비교된다.
GraphQL schema boundary는 GraphQL API의 공개 질의 계약을 형성한다.
resolver data boundary는 N+1 조회 위험을 dataloader 전략과 함께 검토해야 한다.
API versioning은 REST API의 장기 호환성 운영 결정에 속한다.
OpenAPI design-first 확장은 기존 OpenAPI 설계 원칙을 실제 운영 루프로 구체화한다.
DTO validation은 REST API 입력 계약의 일부다.
trace correlation은 Zipkin과 Micrometer 계측 흐름으로 구현된다.
DGS는 GraphQL schema와 resolver 구현을 Spring 런타임에 연결한다.

Rest Resource Modeling Decision는 Rest Resource Modeling Risk를 구체적 구현 선택으로 연결한다.

Rest Resource Modeling Testing는 Rest Resource Modeling Boundary를 인증 흐름과 연결한다.
